title: Panneer's Grace Lies in Keeping Silent!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The Tamil below is a source-checked reading copy: the opening salutation omitted by OCR has been restored, clipped dates and newspaper headlines damaged at page breaks have been completed from the scans, and clear OCR character and word-break errors have been repaired. The canonical source file remains unchanged and authoritative. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: Our Tamil Nadu Chief Minister O. Panneerselvam appears to have forgotten that he is the Chief Minister governing a State. Like the principal of some college, he has issued a statement trying to teach lessons on what a “benami Government” means, what a “minority Government” means, what an “adviser” means and what a “Deputy Chief Minister” means. Whether or not he attends each day to the responsibilities of the Tamil Nadu Government, he never fails to issue statements in “Amma's” way, in “Amma's” style and with “Amma's” approval. He has threatened that if DMK members behave in a manner that deprives members of their rights and brings disgrace upon the Assembly, they will receive the consequences they deserve. The DMK will not fear Panneerselvam's empty rustling. It will discharge its duty properly and according to law. But I have already described at length the many incidents in which AIADMK members behaved in ways that deprived members of their rights and brought disgrace upon the House.

Panneerselvam has also issued this challenge: “The entire country knows how Karunanidhi, unable to face the firm arguments of Revolutionary Leader Amma directly, hid behind files. If he has courage, is Karunanidhi ready to attend the Assembly on the fourth and speak?” Mr Panneerselvam, nothing prevents me from attending the Assembly on the fourth. It requires no particular courage. But what about your “Amma”? You are the one who has compelled me to ask this question! “Should someone living in a glass house try to throw stones?”

Panneerselvam further asks in his statement whether “Karunanidhi believes there is any subject on which to criticise the Tamil Nadu Government in the Assembly.” Not merely in the Assembly, but here before the people's forum, problems concerning the AIADMK Government have piled up like a mountain, one upon another, during these three and a half years. There is no room here to mention them all, so I give a compilation only of what appeared in the newspapers over the past week.
**22 November 2014**
Sagayam files petition in court. (Murasoli)
Three Commissioners transferred in three months at Coimbatore Corporation. (Murasoli)
Ma. Subramanian questions Mayor Duraisamy. (Murasoli)
Monorail project. (The Hindu)
New dam: total shutdown in the delta districts. (Dinakaran)
Are you ready to prove the allegation against Stalin? (Dinakaran)
Metropolitan Transport Corporation loses Rs 3.75 crore a month. (Dinakaran)
Defamation case against Dr Ramadoss. (Dinakaran)
Fine imposed on DSP and Sub-Inspector for failing to protect a woman even after a court order. (Dinakaran)
Case against sixty-nine per cent reservation: hearing adjourned to 1 December. (Dina Thanthi)
**23 November 2014**
Six arrested in case concerning assault on a schoolteacher. (Dinakaran)
Vellore sees its fifth Superintendent of Police in three years. (Dinamalar)
Has Saidai Duraisamy resigned? (Dinamalar)
Public Works Department in disarray; irrigation schemes stalled. (Dinamalar)
Conflicting views over Vijay Pingale's transfer. (Dinamalar)
Gold for the *thali*: poor people dissatisfied. (Dinakaran)
Ruling-party interference in the Highways Department. (Dinakaran)
Sembakkam DMK councillor attacked. (Dina Thanthi)
“7 days, 9 Murders; is the City Safe?” (The Hindu)
“Rumours of Mayor's quitting spark flurry of Calls.” (The Hindu)
“DMK AIADMK workers clash near Tambaram.” (The Hindu)
**24 November 2014**
Report on mineral sand. (Murasoli)
Assistance grant for persons with disabilities. (Murasoli)
Fourteen Tamil Nadu fishermen detained. (Dinamani)
Fare concession on government buses: persons with disabilities complain. (Dinamani)
Tirunelveli and Thoothukudi turning into killing fields. (Dinamalar)
Loot in fly-ash sales. (Dinamalar)
Water levels in Chennai's drinking-water reservoirs cause concern. (Dinamalar)
Sudhakaran's friend arrested in cheating case. (Dinakaran)
Despite continuous rain in Chennai, reservoir levels have not risen. (Dinakaran)
Vacancies in the Transport Corporation: applicants shocked. (Dinakaran)
Transport Corporation charges passengers excessive fares. (Dinakaran)
When will action be taken against officers involved in the Aavin irregularities? (Dinakaran)
Who will become Engineer-in-Chief of Tamil Nadu's Water Resources Department? (Dinakaran)
Mekedatu dam: Prime Minister must be met, says Dr Ramadoss. (Dinakaran)
**25 November 2014**
High Court on J.'s portrait in government offices. (Dinamalar)
Union Government fails to provide Rs 637 crore: Electricity Board officials travel to Delhi. (Dinamalar)
Anganwadi worker interview suddenly cancelled; road blockade follows. (Dinamalar)
Fertiliser shortage in Tamil Nadu. (Dinamalar)
O.P.S., the Chief Minister with no inaugurations. (Dinamalar)
Metro Rail company challenges Rs 10 crore registration fee. (Dina Thanthi)
Corporation identifies 132 dangerous buildings. (Dinamalar)
Issue of modern guides at museum stopped. (Dinamani)
Contractor cheated of Rs 1.29 crore: complaint against former Minister Pachaimaal. (Dinamani)
Woman attempts self-immolation in protest against police inaction. (Dinamani)
Police baton-charge villagers opposing sand quarry. (Dinamani)
**26 November 2014**
What became of statements made under Rule 110? asks DMK Treasurer M.K. Stalin. (Murasoli)
“Tape” concerning Saidai Duraisamy. (Murasoli)
Corporation disregards order, says High Court. (Dinakaran)
City buses break down midway. (Dinamani)
Rainwater-harvesting schemes go to waste. (Dinamalar)
Guideline value of houses increased. (Dinamalar)
Chit-fund fraud at the Secretariat. (Dinamalar)
Medical students struggle without training stipend for six months. (Dinamalar)
Registration numbers of eleven thousand traders cancelled. (Dinamalar)
Does construction continue even after notice? asks High Court. (Dinamalar)
Misappropriation of panchayat funds: two secretaries suspended. (Dinamalar)
What happened to the mineral-sand inquiry report? (Dinamalar)
Tamil Nadu Government's free mega-size slippers. (Dinamalar)
**27 November 2014**
CPI(M) demands CBI inquiry into corruption in procurement of nutritious-meal eggs. (Dinakaran)
Blood vomited in police station after beatings and kicks. (Dinakaran)
Registration Department lost Rs 1,166 crore in revenue last year after failing to meet its target. (Dinakaran)
Corporation roads. (Dinakaran)
Police baton-charge Sri Lankan refugees in Pudukkottai. (Dinakaran)
Work slows at Madurai Collectorate. (Dinakaran)
AIADMK area secretary clashes with Sub-Inspector at police station. (Dinakaran)
Fifteen-year-old girl alone at home raped in Medavakkam. (Malai Malar)
Policeman's death in Nagapattinam: wife seeks CB-CID inquiry. (Malai Malar)
Twenty-five huts destroyed in midnight fire at Tiruvottiyur. (Malai Malar)
Minister Ramana's relative murdered: panchayat president detained under Goondas Act. (Malai Malar)
Leopard beats and drags away youth Srinivasan near Sathyamangalam, killing him. (Malai Malar)
DMK councillor assaulted at Salem Corporation meeting. (Tamil Murasu)
AIADMK MLA abuses officials who came to clear encroachment in Bhavani. (Tamil Murasu)
Three teachers transferred over sexual-harassment complaint in Virudhunagar. (Tamil Murasu)
Jewellery worth Rs 1 crore stolen in Dindigul. (20 November)
Engineer Sivachandran's throat slit near Arani. (20 November)
Chain snatched from Chennai college professor. (20 November)
Woman killed for jewellery in Vepery. (22 November)
Thirty sovereigns of jewellery stolen in Thiruverkadu. (22 November)
Auto stopped in Chennai; traders attacked and robbed. (23 November)
Four-sovereign chain snatched in Ambattur. (23 November)
Sixty sovereigns stolen in Pulianthope. (23 November)
Auto intercepted near airport; US dollars worth Rs 80 lakh stolen. (23 November)
Jewellery worth Rs 2 crore stolen from Coimbatore finance company. (24 November)
Lock broken and computers worth Rs 8 lakh stolen in Chennai. (25 November)
Retired bank officer murdered in Tiruchi. (25 November)
Woman murdered in Kolathur. (25 November)
Disabled man murdered near Tirunelveli. (25 November)
One hundred and ten sovereigns of jewellery stolen from woman Sub-Inspector's house. (26 November)
Lock broken and thirty sovereigns stolen from private-company officer's home in Chennai. (26 November)
Fifteen sovereigns of gold jewellery and Rs 20,000 stolen from businessman's Triplicane home. (26 November)
Woman employee tied up and jewellery stolen at private hospital in New Washermenpet. (26 November)
Two women raped and murdered in Salem on the same day. (26 November)
Call-taxi driver murdered at Keerapakkam. (26 November)
Rs 2.60 lakh stolen from bus passenger in Chennai. (26 November)
Jewellery snatched at three places in Tambaram and Pallavaram. (27 November)
Five-sovereign chain snatched from woman in Avadi. (27 November)
Farmer stabbed to death at ration shop. (27 November)
**28 November 2014**
Chennai Corporation's DMK members submit complaint against Mayor to Home Secretary. (Murasoli)
Fifty people vomit and faint after eating at canteen near Avadi. (Dinakaran)
Tamil Nadu officials denied permission to visit Mullaperiyar dam. (Dinakaran)
Land acquisition for Armed Police: High Court directs DGP to pay Rs 10.62 crore compensation. (Dinakaran)
Bail denied to eight, including AIADMK functionary, in Aavin milk case. (Dinakaran)
Power generation falls; power cuts continue. (Dinamalar)
108 emergency-ambulance employees fast. (Dinamalar)
Pottery industry declining: will Government act? (Dinamalar)
Information on water storage in dams and lakes concealed. (Dinamalar)
Death certificates for the living: anything available for money. (Dinamalar)
Hindu Munnani functionary attacked. (Dinamalar)
Three children die in one day at Vellore hospital. (Dinamalar)
Rs 1 crore misappropriated in Nagapattinam district. (Dinamalar)
Fourteen sovereigns stolen in Ayanavaram house burglary. (Dinamalar)
Farmer Kamatchi stabbed to death near Nilakottai. (Dinamalar)
Homes of Secretariat officers Bhanumathi and Geetha burgled in Peters Colony. (Dinamalar)

Mr Panneerselvam, you asked us to point out any shortcomings of this Government that could be discussed in the Assembly. As a result, I have compiled and shown you this list of so many irregularities under this Government in the space of just one week. Is this list enough? Or do you want details month by month and year by year? There is abundant material on the miseries of AIADMK rule, enough to speak about day after day. Why open your mouth, invite trouble and pay the price for it? Just as you remained until becoming Chief Minister, quietness is what becomes Panneer; it is also good for the country! Therefore, Panneer's grace lies in keeping silent!
With affection,
M.K.
30 November 2014
